children.text: submit an application for amounts under this part; and
perform the duties specified in subsection (f) and, if applicable, subsection (g).

children.heading: No requirement to be a legal entity
children.label: b No requirement to be a legal entity
children.text: An entity may be established to serve as a collaborative applicant under this section without being a legal entity.

children.heading: Remedial action
children.label: c Remedial action
children.text: If the Secretary finds that a collaborative applicant for a geographic area does not meet the requirements of this section, or if there is no collaborative applicant for a geographic area, the Secretary may take remedial action to ensure fair distribution of grant amounts under part C to eligible entities within that area. Such measures may include designating another body as a collaborative applicant, or permitting other eligible entities to apply directly for grants.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: d Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to displace conflict of interest or government fair practices laws, or their equivalent, that govern applicants for grant amounts under parts B and C.

children.label: A
children.text: apply for a grant under ;section 11382(c) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: receive and distribute grant funds awarded under part C; and

children.label: C
children.text: perform other administrative duties.

children.heading: Retention of duties
children.label: 2 Retention of duties
children.text: Any collaborative applicant that designates an agent pursuant to paragraph (1) shall regardless of such designation retain all of its duties and responsibilities under this subchapter.

children.label: i
children.text: the program requirements under ; andsection 11386 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the selection criteria described under ; andsection 11386a of this title

children.label: B
children.text: to establish priorities for funding projects in the geographic area involved;
participate in the Consolidated Plan for the geographic area served by the collaborative applicant; and

children.label: A
children.text: collects unduplicated counts of individuals and families experiencing homelessness;

children.label: B
children.text: analyzes patterns of use of assistance provided under parts B and C for the geographic area involved;

children.label: C
children.text: provides information to project sponsors and applicants for needs analyses and funding priorities; and

children.label: i
children.text: encryption of data collected for purposes of HMIS;

children.label: ii
children.text: documentation, including keeping an accurate accounting, proper usage, and disclosure, of HMIS data;

children.label: iii
children.text: access to HMIS data by staff, contractors, law enforcement, and academic researchers;

children.label: iv
children.text: rights of persons receiving services under this subchapter;

children.label: v
children.text: criminal and civil penalties for unlawful disclosure of data; and

children.label: vi
children.text: such other standards as may be determined necessary by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: applies to undertake such collection and distribution responsibilities in an application submitted under this part; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is selected to perform such responsibilities by the Secretary; or

children.label: I
children.text: has the capacity to perform such responsibilities; and

children.label: II
children.text: would serve the purposes of this chapter as they apply to the geographic area; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary provides the collaborative applicant with the technical assistance necessary to perform such responsibilities as such assistance is agreed to by the collaborative applicant.

children.heading: Required actions by a unified funding agency
children.label: 2 Required actions by a unified funding agency
A
children.text: require each project sponsor who is funded by a grant received under part C to establish such fiscal control and fund accounting procedures as may be necessary to assure the proper disbursal of, and accounting for, Federal funds awarded to the project sponsor under part C in order to ensure that all financial transactions carried out under part C are conducted, and records maintained, in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles; and

children.label: B
children.text: arrange for an annual survey, audit, or evaluation of the financial records of each project carried out by a project sponsor funded by a grant received under part C.

children.heading: Conflict of interest
children.label: h Conflict of interest
children.text: No board member of a collaborative applicant may participate in decisions of the collaborative applicant concerning the award of a grant, or provision of other financial benefits, to such member or the organization that such member represents.
